Data.Name: Sneaker Wall
Data.Description: A booth wall of roller shoes and platform soles. One orange micro-wheel display pair is missing, and the dust gap is fresh enough to draw a clean oval around it.
Data.FlavorText: Brick's kind of evidence: nobody remembers faces, but everyone remembers soles.

Data.Name: Zine Table
Data.Description: Six numbered proof slots sit under rain-speckled acrylic. Slot seven is hand-labeled in Jun's violet ink: MIKO FINAL - HOLD UNTIL DROP. The slot is empty.
Data.FlavorText: The table does not accuse anyone. It just leaves a perfect hole where the night should make sense.

Data.Name: Mirror Column
Data.Description: A tri-fold mirror column used for fit checks. In the corner reflection, Sera's sticker camera caught orange wheels cutting toward the backlot curtain while a second hand lifted a page from the zine table.
Data.FlavorText: The mirror remembers two crimes pretending to be one.

Data.Name: Canal Catwalk Mat
Data.Description: A wet vinyl runway mat printed with L-E-CAT sponsor marks. Orange wheel tracks cross it twice: once toward the market booths, once back toward the print curtain after the proof vanished.
Data.FlavorText: Every dramatic exit leaves a receipt when the floor is wet.

Data.Name: Sound Cart
Data.Description: A portable speaker cart still looping the pre-drop playlist. Its cue log marks a hard rack-clatter at 20:17, then a roller brake squeal exactly twelve seconds later.
Data.FlavorText: The crowd heard bass. The cart heard timing.

Data.Name: Screen Press
Data.Description: Jun's press is still warm. A ghost image under the mesh shows Miko's look with a rival crew stamp burned into the corner, as if someone prepared a counterfeit reveal before the original drop.
Data.FlavorText: Ink does not panic. Printers do.

Data.Name: Dye Sink
Data.Description: The sink is ringed with teal rinse water, cherry-static perfume, and orange wheel dust. Someone cleaned fabric here too quickly and left the drain smelling like Miko's bag.
Data.FlavorText: A cover-up with good taste is still a cover-up.

Data.Name: Rolling Rack
Data.Description: A maze of rolling garment racks shoved just far enough to make a blind corridor. A torn rival page is tucked behind the magenta samples, creased by a glove with brass ring marks.
Data.FlavorText: The best hiding place in fashion is between two things everyone pretends to admire.

Data.Name: Delivery Door
Data.Description: The back door's scanner log shows one delivery badge at 20:12 and a manual wedge four minutes later. The wedge mark is low, where a roller shoe could kick it open.
Data.FlavorText: Someone wanted a way out before anyone knew there was a chase.

Data.Name: Miko's Holo Sling
Data.Description: A mirror-bright sling bag stuffed with look tags, pins, and enough tape to survive rain.
Data.FlavorText: Everything important hangs close to the ribs.

Data.Name: Cherry Static Atomizer
Data.Description: A tiny spray-can perfume that makes fabric smell like wet neon and sweet smoke.

Data.Name: Folded Lookbook Proof
Data.Description: Miko's folded proof: six planned pages, Jun's violet registration marks, and a penciled note where page seven should not exist: DO NOT PRINT UNTIL SERA CLEARS TIMESTAMP.

Data.Name: Sticker Flash Compact
Data.Description: A sticker-covered camera used to prove who wore what first. Its last thumbnail is a warped mirror reflection: Nox rolling away, and another sleeve still at the zine table.

Data.Name: Rival Lookbook Page
Data.Description: A torn rival lookbook page with Miko's fit, printed twelve hours too early. The registration marks match Jun's screen press, but the corner stamp belongs to a rival crew that is not supposed to have booth access.

Data.Name: Transit Bracelet Stack
Data.Description: Friendship bracelets braided around a transit tag. It scans, but only if you look good asking.

Data.Name: Market Sample Atomizer
Data.Description: A sample fragrance from a booth that promises confidence and plausible deniability.

Data.Name: Static Pin Beanie
Data.Description: A black ribbed beanie pinned with tiny smiling charms and one cracked route token.

Data.Name: Magenta Wraparound Shades
Data.Description: Shield-bright glasses that make eye contact feel choreographed.

Data.Name: Black Graffiti Crop Hoodie
Data.Description: Soft black hoodie panels marked with yellow and teal handstyle.

Data.Name: Iridescent Vinyl Bomber
Data.Description: A cropped bomber that throws coral, teal, and orange back at every sign.

Data.Name: Ring-Laced Fingerless Gloves
Data.Description: Lace gloves with brass ring loops for pinning fabric on the move.

Data.Name: Patchwork Cargo Skirt
Data.Description: A layered cargo skirt stitched over black leggings with chain flashes.

Data.Name: Teal Sole Platforms
Data.Description: Platform sneakers loud enough to count as a location ping.

Data.Name: Transit Friendship Stack
Data.Description: Bracelets and a transit tag from three boroughs and four almost-friends.

Data.Name: Lace Sync Subdermal Tag
Data.Description: A tiny style-auth implant that timestamps fit changes for L-E-CAT receipts.

Data.Name: Blue Edge Rain Lenses
Data.Description: Clear street lenses with blue edging and zero patience for fake seams.

Data.Name: Blue Lace Mesh Top
Data.Description: A soft mesh layer built to glow under shop signage.

Data.Name: Transparent Rain Poncho
Data.Description: A glossy clear poncho with electric blue piping and visible stitch channels.

Data.Name: Tailor Grip Gloves
Data.Description: Fingerless gloves with needle-safe grip pads and brass ring marks.

Data.Name: Glass-Silver Trousers
Data.Description: Wide reflective trousers that turn puddles into stage lights.

Data.Name: Orange Wheel Roller Shoes
Data.Description: White roller shoes with orange micro-wheels and terrible brakes.

Data.Name: Charm LED Choker
Data.Description: A black choker ticking magenta LEDs like a tiny runway timer.

Data.Name: Scuffed Pin Beanie
Data.Description: A soft beanie loaded with enamel pins from shops that closed years ago.

Data.Name: Route-Map Tee
Data.Description: A black tee marked with a courier route only Brick can read.

Data.Name: Yellow Graffiti Hoodie
Data.Description: An oversized hoodie with yellow shell panels and purple spray scars.

Data.Name: Box-Cutter Grip Gloves
Data.Description: Fingerless lace gloves upgraded with courier tape and ring pulls.

Data.Name: Wide-Leg Cargo Trousers
Data.Description: Reflective cargos cut wide for running through packed stalls.

Data.Name: Teal Sole Runner Platforms
Data.Description: Chunky teal-soled runners with a suspiciously quiet tread.

Data.Name: Transit Tag Bracelet Stack
Data.Description: A bracelet stack that doubles as a neighborhood passport.

Data.Name: Sera's Magenta Visor
Data.Description: Wide magenta glasses polished clean enough to reflect bad ideas.

Data.Name: Black Camera Mesh
Data.Description: A dark mesh layer with strap pads where cameras rub holes in lesser fabric.

Data.Name: Pink Flash Vinyl Jacket
Data.Description: A black and pink jacket cut for squatting beside puddles and catching angles.

Data.Name: Shutter Lace Gloves
Data.Description: Fingerless gloves with brass rings for stable shutter taps.

Data.Name: Photo Pit Patch Skirt
Data.Description: Patchwork skirt panels weighted so they do not blow into the lens.

Data.Name: Low Noise Roller Shoes
Data.Description: Tiny orange wheels let Sera drift for video without shaking the shot.

Data.Name: Sticker Flash Camera
Data.Description: A compact camera covered in proof-of-scene stickers.

Data.Name: Nox's Black Pin Beanie
Data.Description: A black beanie whose pins look cheerful until you read the scratches.

Data.Name: Smoked Magenta Wraps
Data.Description: Magenta visor shades with smoked edges and a competitive glare.

Data.Name: Charcoal Graffiti Hoodie
Data.Description: A black hoodie layered with orange tags and borrowed slogans.

Data.Name: Backlot Clear Poncho
Data.Description: Transparent rainwear meant to show off what it refuses to protect.

Data.Name: Pinch Lace Gloves
Data.Description: Gloves nimble enough for clasps, tags, and quick theft.

Data.Name: Orange Patch Cargo Layers
Data.Description: Patchwork cargos cut asymmetrical, expensive, and hard to chase.

Data.Name: Backlot Roller Shoes
Data.Description: Orange-wheeled shoes for leaving with style before blame arrives.

Data.Name: Borrowed Scent Atomizer
Data.Description: A spray-can atomizer from someone else's bag.

Data.Name: Printer Glasses
Data.Description: Round glasses flecked with violet screen-print dust.

Data.Name: Mint Print Hoodie
Data.Description: A mint and violet hoodie with ink ghosts along the sleeves.

Data.Name: Ink-Safe Clear Poncho
Data.Description: A clear poncho worn over wet prints and nervous shoulders.

Data.Name: Ink-Stained Lace Gloves
Data.Description: Fingerless gloves stained in five editions of the same mistake.

Data.Name: Soft Reflective Trousers
Data.Description: Wide silver trousers patched where the dryer tried to eat them.

Data.Name: Mint Platform Sneakers
Data.Description: Teal platform sneakers with paper dust in the sole grooves.

Data.Name: Tabbed Proof Zine
Data.Description: A folded zine with colored tabs and one page that changes the whole night.

Data.Name: Backlot Curtain
Data.Description: A black vinyl curtain behind the market booths. Orange wheel dust collects along the bottom edge, and the tie has been snapped instead of untied.
Data.FlavorText: The curtain is not hiding the backlot. It is hiding who knew to use it.

Data.Name: Market Curtain
Data.Description: The return curtain opens toward the market lights. From this side, the vinyl is scratched by rack wheels and smeared with cherry-static perfume.
Data.FlavorText: Back to the crowd, back to everyone pretending they saw nothing.

Data.Name: Token Lab Holdout
Data.Description: A compact test sidearm for checking combat highlighting.
Data.FlavorText: Only here to make the orange easier to see.

Data.Name: Token Lab Magazine
Data.Description: A loaded magazine for the token lab holdout.
Data.FlavorText: A tiny stack of highly practical test data.
